reasoning: The Southwest Division is currently a two-team race between the San Antonio Spurs and the Houston Rockets, as the Grizzlies, Mavericks, and Pelicans are all performing poorly (e.g., Mavericks and Grizzlies are bottom-dwellers in 2026). As of late January 2026, the Spurs were 31-15 while the Rockets were 28-16, giving the Spurs a 2.5 game lead. The betting market estimated the Rockets at 0.13 to win the division on February 19. Recent news from late February highlights the Spurs playing exceptionally well—including a historic stretch of winning four straight road games by 25+ points and a dominant win-loss run. With the Spurs extending their lead and fewer games remaining in the season (about 6 weeks left), the probability of the Rockets overcoming the deficit has slightly decreased since the February 19 market estimate. Consequently, assigning an 11% probability is reasonable.
